meta.name: Orange-Chili Pork
meta.subtitle: Sticky-savory citrus glaze with gentle heat (stovetop or oven finish)

summary: Tender pork cooked quickly and finished in a glossy orange-chili glaze. Great over rice, with roasted vegetables, or tucked into tacos/bowls.

yield.notes: Scale easily; keep glaze ratio the same.

optional.label: Wire rack
optional.notes: Useful if you choose an oven finish for crisp edges.

ingredients.label: Pork tenderloin (sliced into medallions) or boneless pork chops (cut into bite-size)
notes: Tenderloin cooks fastest and stays tender.
If using thicker chops, pound lightly for even cooking.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Go lighter if your soy sauce is very salty.

ingredients.label: Honey (or brown sugar)
ingredients.notes: Honey gives a glossy finish; brown sugar gives deeper caramel notes.

ingredients.label: Fresh ginger, grated
ingredients.notes: Or 1 tsp ground ginger in a pinch.

ingredients.label: Chili paste (or red pepper flakes)
ingredients.notes: Start with 1 tsp for mild-medium; increase to 2 tsp for hotter.

steps.title: Mix the orange-chili glaze
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together orange juice, orange zest, soy sauce, rice vinegar (or lime juice), honey (or brown sugar), garlic, ginger, and chili paste/flakes.
If using cornstarch: whisk cornstarch with water to make a smooth slurry; set aside separately.
checks: Glaze tastes balanced: salty-sweet with orange brightness
Cornstarch slurry is smooth (no lumps) if using

steps.title: Season the pork
instructions: Pat pork dry and cut into medallions (tenderloin) or bite-size pieces (chops).
Season with salt and black pepper.

steps.title: Sear the pork
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add neutral oil.
Add pork in a single layer and sear until browned, 2–3 minutes per side (work in batches if needed).
Transfer browned pork to a plate (it will finish cooking in the glaze).
checks: Pork has browned edges (don’t worry if not fully cooked through yet)
Pan has browned bits (fond) but nothing burnt

steps.title: Reduce the glaze and finish cooking
instructions: Lower heat to medium. Pour glaze into the skillet and scrape up browned bits.
Return pork (and any juices) to the pan and cook 2–4 minutes, stirring/turning to coat, until pork is cooked through.
If using the cornstarch slurry: whisk it again, then drizzle into simmering glaze and stir 30–60 seconds until thickened and glossy.

temperature.notes: 145°F / 63°C is a commonly referenced safe finished temp for whole cuts of pork.

checks: Glaze coats the back of a spoon (or thickens quickly if slurry used)
Pork is just cooked through and still juicy

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Taste the glaze. Add a squeeze of lime for extra brightness or a tiny pinch of salt if needed.
Top with green onions and sesame seeds (optional).
Serve over rice, quinoa, or roasted vegetables; also great in bowls or tacos.

refrigerator.notes: Glaze keeps pork moist; store tightly covered.

freezer.notes: Freeze up to 2 months. Thaw overnight in fridge; reheat gently.

methods: gentle skillet with splash of water
reheat.notes: Avoid high heat to prevent pork from drying out.

handling: Keep raw pork refrigerated until cooking.
Wash hands, board, and knife after handling raw pork.

internalTemp.notes: Rest briefly after cooking if needed; carryover heat will finish.

variations.label: Extra spicy orange-chili pork
notes: Add 1 minced jalapeño when simmering the glaze.

variations.label: Ginger-forward version
notes: Use 1 tbsp grated ginger and finish with extra orange zest.

variations.label: Oven-finished for sticky edges
notes: After searing pork, pour glaze over pork in an oven-safe skillet or baking dish.
Bake at 425°F / 218°C for 8–10 minutes, basting once, until glossy and cooked through.

weeknightNotes: Prep glaze while pan heats, then sear and finish in under 20 minutes.
Serve with microwave rice or pre-roasted veggies for speed.
